summary: The 22nd National Assembly election functioned as a mid-term referendum on the Yoon Suk-yeol administration. Persistent low approval (stuck in the mid-20s through the campaign), a chain of named governance controversies (Chae Sang-byeol investigation-pressure allegation, Itaewon disaster response, 2023 Jamboree mismanagement, Busan Expo bid failure, and first-lady Kim Keon-hee matters), cost-of-living frustration, and consolidated opposition messaging — amplified by record advance-voting turnout (31.28%, all-time high for a general election) — drove a decisive Democratic-led victory. In Seoul, Democrats took 37 of 48 constituency seats; the People Power Party held the Gangnam-Seocho-Songpa cluster plus Yongsan and Dongjak 을.

issues.label: Government approval
issues.description: Mid-term evaluation of the Yoon administration; approval stuck in the 20% range, widely framed as voters punishing 'incompetence and irresponsibility.'

issues.label: Governance controversies
issues.description: Chain of named incidents: Chae Sang-byeol investigation-pressure allegation, Itaewon disaster response, 2023 Jamboree mismanagement, Busan Expo bid failure, and Kim Keon-hee first-lady matters.

issues.label: Opposition consolidation
issues.description: Democratic Party + Democratic Alliance, reinforced by the Cho Kuk Innovation / Rebuilding Korea party (12 PR seats). Satellite-party structures maximized proportional allocation.

issues.label: Inflation and cost of living
issues.description: Rising inflation shaped voter mood; the 'green onion incident' (president's 875-won claim vs 3–4× real prices) crystallized perceived detachment in English coverage; Korean framing subsumed this under the broader 'incompetence' narrative.

issues.label: Third-party breakthroughs
issues.description: Cho Kuk Innovation Party emerged as second opposition force without any constituency candidates; Reform Party (Lee Jun-seok) and New Future (Lee Nak-yeon faction) added minor-party breakthroughs. Signals opposition coalition breadth, not a unitary wave.
